steps.action: Fandango is a lively partner dance originating i with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, accent beat.
steps.text: Fandango is a lively partner dance originating i with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, accent beat.

steps.action: Later it took the 3-4 tempo, and the characteris
steps.text: Later it took the 3-4 tempo, and the characteristic Spanish rhythm.

steps.action: Soon after its first introduction, in the 17th c
steps.text: Soon after its first introduction, in the 17th century, it was condemned by the ecclesiastical authorities in Spain as a "godless dance".

steps.action: Two celebrated dancers were accordingly introduc
steps.text: Two celebrated dancers were accordingly introduced to perform the fandango before the Consistory.

steps.action: This they did with such effect, that, according
steps.text: This they did with such effect, that, according to the old chronicler, "every one joined in, and the hall of the consistorium was turned into a dancing saloon".

steps.action: Camille Saint-Saëns' "Danse Macabre" also follow
steps.text: Camille Saint-Saëns' "Danse Macabre" also follows the rhythm of the fandango.

steps.action: Italian composer Domenico Scarlatti, who was inf
steps.text: Italian composer Domenico Scarlatti, who was influenced by Iberian folk music, had several passages reminiscent of fandango, such as in his keyboard sonata K.

steps.action: The fandangos grandes (big fandangos) are normal
steps.text: The fandangos grandes (big fandangos) are normally danced by couples, which start out slowly with gradually increasing tempo.

steps.action: The dancers can be boy and girl, boy and boy (mo
steps.text: The dancers can be boy and girl, boy and boy (most frequent) or, rarely, two girls.

steps.action: While one of the dancers dances, the other just
steps.text: While one of the dancers dances, the other just "goes along".

steps.action: Afterwards, they "both drag their feet for a whi
steps.text: Afterwards, they "both drag their feet for a while" until the other one takes his turn.

steps.action: The dance is usually performed by two Campinos.
steps.text: The dance is usually performed by two Campinos.

steps.action: As local musicians perform the Son Jarocho music
steps.text: As local musicians perform the Son Jarocho music, people dance "zapateado" atop a large wooden platform known as a Tarima.

steps.action: The dance was first popularized by the upper cla
steps.text: The dance was first popularized by the upper class Illustrados and was later adapted by the local masses, becoming a staple of rural Filipino folk dances.

steps.action: Spelled as "Pandanggo" in Filipino languages, th
steps.text: Spelled as "Pandanggo" in Filipino languages, the dance branched into different local variations.

steps.action: The Fandango was also used as musical basis for
steps.text: The Fandango was also used as musical basis for other Traditional Philippine folk dances, including Cariñosa and Pandanggo Rinconada from Nabua, Camarines Sur.

steps.action: The Fandango was also utilized in Religious cele
steps.text: The Fandango was also utilized in Religious celebrations and devotional dances, such as the Obando Fertility Rites, and the Pandangguhan of Pateros in honor of Saint Martha.

steps.action: Nettl: The Story of Dance Music (New York, 1947)
steps.text: Nettl: The Story of Dance Music (New York, 1947) B.

steps.action: Fandango can both be sung and danced.
steps.text: Fandango can both be sung and danced.

steps.action: Sung fandango is usually bipartite: it has an in
steps.text: Sung fandango is usually bipartite: it has an instrumental introduction followed by "variaciones".

steps.action: Sung fandango usually follows the structure of "
steps.text: Sung fandango usually follows the structure of "cante" that consist of four or five octosyllabic verses (coplas) or musical phrases (tercios).

steps.action: Occasionally, the first copla is repeated.
steps.text: Occasionally, the first copla is repeated.

steps.action: The meter of fandango is similar to that of the
steps.text: The meter of fandango is similar to that of the bolero and seguidilla.

steps.action: It was originally notated in 68 time, of slow te
steps.text: It was originally notated in 68 time, of slow tempo, mostly in the minor, with a trio in the major; sometimes, however, the whole was in a major key.
